name: sidecar
description: Battle-tested guardrails for Claude Code, ported from the hexa-native agent wilson.

plugins.name: wilson-guards
plugins.description: Three dancinlab-workflow PreToolUse guards in one bundle, standalone ports of wilson governance + guard-* predicates: ssot-lock (deny editing a file matched by an ssot-lock: bullet in the nearest AGENTS.md ## Governance), tape-append-only (a .log.tape event-history file is append-only — deny a rewriting Edit or an overwriting Write; per the tape v1.2 architecture-vs-history split a plain .tape is editable architecture, so the guard is inert for it), domain-lint (a root UPPERCASE.md topic roadmap must be Head + --- + ## Log). Each guard inert unless its convention is present. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_GUARDS=1.

plugins.name: wilson-ssot
plugins.description: AGENTS.md walk-up SSOT injected at SessionStart / UserPromptSubmit (wilson agents-md plugin equivalent).

plugins.name: wilson-readme-format
plugins.description: Deny Write/Edit on a repo-root README.md that violates the readme-format anti-patterns (multi-glyph H1, non-English At-a-glance fence, forbidden #### heading). Standalone port of wilson's guard-readme-format — no wilson binary dependency. Ships a companion `docs-convention` skill teaching the README/UPPERCASE.md roadmap conventions up front — also covers wilson-guards/domain-lint.

plugins.name: wilson-prefs
plugins.description: User preferences (reply language / code language / response style) set via the /wilson-prefs:prefs slash command, persisted to plugin data, injected as context. SessionStart / PostCompact inject the full `## Prefs` block (PostCompact lands in the clean post-summary context, surviving compaction); every Nth UserPromptSubmit turn (`refresh-every`, default 10) re-injects the full body, and every other turn injects the compact always-on micro-spec so the style never decays to a bare label between refreshes. With an explicit reply language the contradictory `_common` language-tracking section is stripped before injection and the `## Prefs` directive lines are localized to that language. Standalone port of wilson prefs — working. Injects nothing until the user sets a preference.

plugins.name: wilson-hexa-verify
plugins.description: PreToolUse: deny Bash invocations of non-hexa verifiers (sympy / PyPhi / wolframscript / mathematica / mathkernel) and redirect to the hexa CLI. PostToolUse: when `hexa verify` reports a new SUPPORTED equation, open a PR to dancinlab/hexa-lang baking the equation into the binary built-in atlas (fills `hexa atlas promote`'s stubbed pr step; PR left for human review, never auto-merged), falling back to prompting the worktree-pr workflow. Standalone port + extension of wilson guard-hexa-verify — working. INERT unless the `hexa` binary is on PATH.

plugins.name: wilson-dangerous-path
plugins.description: Deny Write/Edit/MultiEdit targeting a protected system path (/etc /usr /bin /sbin /System /.git /.gnupg) or a credential path (~/.ssh, ~/.aws, gh config, keychain, credentials). Standalone port of wilson guard-dangerous-path — working.

plugins.name: wilson-git-guard
plugins.description: Deny force-push at the PreToolUse layer — a Bash `git push` carrying --force / -f / a +refspec (and --force-with-lease, unless SIDECAR_ALLOW_FORCE_WITH_LEASE=1) is blocked. Standalone port of wilson's git-guard. Ships a companion `git-workflow` skill teaching the safe-push convention up front so the force-push deny rarely has to fire.

plugins.name: wilson-secret-guard
plugins.description: Block live secrets before they land. PreToolUse (Write/Edit/MultiEdit): deny writing a real .env file or any content carrying a high-confidence credential (AWS / GitHub / GitLab / Anthropic / OpenAI / Slack / Google / Stripe tokens, PEM private keys). UserPromptSubmit: block a prompt that pastes such a credential. High-confidence patterns only — near-zero false positives. Ships a companion `security` skill (wilson-sdlc-style) teaching the secret/path/destructive-shell convention up front so the deny rarely has to fire — also covers the sibling wilson-dangerous-path & wilson-bash-guard surface.

plugins.name: wilson-bash-guard
plugins.description: Deny catastrophic Bash commands at PreToolUse — pipe-to-shell from a network downloader (curl|sh), `rm -rf` of a root/home path, fork bombs, disk destroyers (dd of=/dev/disk, mkfs, >/dev/sd*), and recursive chmod/chown on / ~ or `.`. High-confidence destructive patterns only — near-zero false positives. Complements wilson-git-guard (force-push) and wilson-dangerous-path (protected-path writes). Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_BASH_GUARD=1.

plugins.name: wilson-output-trim
plugins.description: Token economy: PreToolUse rewrites Bash commands (updatedInput) so stdout passes a TF-IDF line-salience + MinHash near-dup filter before the model ingests it. Spirit-port of wilson compaction-prefilter — working. Small output passes verbatim; exit code preserved via pipefail.

plugins.name: wilson-pool
plugins.description: Route heavy Bash commands to a remote host roster via ssh (PreToolUse updatedInput) + /wilson-pool:pool config + SessionStart ## Pool block. Each host carries a platform tag (macOS-only / Linux-only commands go to a matching host, else round-robined) and an optional per-host workdir override. workdir may be explicit or `auto` (mirror the current project — local ~/<rel> → remote ~/<rel>). `autosync on` rsyncs the local project to the target host before each routed command — a continuously-changing tree stays fresh with zero manual sync, and the workdir is created if absent (additive by default; `autosync mirror` adds rsync --delete); with `autosync off` a pre-flight `test -d` check skips routing to any host lacking the workdir — but a connection failure (ssh transport error / timeout) is never cached as `missing`, so a transient network blip skips that one command only and never benches the host for the session (the host always stays in pool.json — removal is the explicit `rm`/`off` commands only). A PreToolUse guard denies a direct Write/Edit/MultiEdit to pool.json — the roster is mutated only through the /wilson-pool:pool command, never hand-edited. A host may also carry a `sudo` tag — root-needing commands (apt, dpkg, yum, dnf, pacman, systemctl, ldconfig, setcap) are routable on their own and are auto-prefixed with `sudo` on a sudo-tagged host (which asserts passwordless sudo). Off-LAN external access via tailscale: optional `transport` knob (global + per-host; default `auto` = `tailscale ssh` when a local tailscale daemon is up else plain `ssh`), routed command `bash -lc`-wrapped for non-login-shell PATH; the autosync rsync path stays plain-ssh in v1 (tailscale-only host → use `autosync off`). CLI: `tailnet` lists tailnet nodes, `add <node> [tailscale|ssh]` auto-detects platform from the tailnet + probes, `status` adds a live reachability probe on top of `show`. Spirit-port of wilson's pool roster. Routing is OFF until the roster has >=1 host AND workdir is set; only Bash is routed.

plugins.name: wilson-checkpoint
plugins.description: Never lose work to a usage limit / rate-limit / crash. A CC hook cannot see the limit event, so this keeps an always-fresh non-destructive snapshot instead: on Stop / PreCompact / SessionEnd it runs `git stash create` (a dangling commit — working tree, index and branches untouched), pins it under refs/wilson-checkpoint/, and writes a resume note; SessionStart re-injects an unconsumed snapshot so the next session continues. Restore/list/clear via /wilson-checkpoint:checkpoint (restore is printed, never auto-applied). Debounced; git-only. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_CHECKPOINT=1.

plugins.name: wilson-gpu
plugins.description: Rented-GPU cost guardrail + lifecycle for RunPod / Vast.ai. SessionStart surfaces every still-billing instance (uptime + estimated cost so far) so a forgotten pod never leaks money; /wilson-gpu down is the kill switch and attach wires an instance into the wilson-pool roster. Operating strategies: watch (visibility), budget ($ ceiling escalation), idle-reaper (flag past max-hours), ephemeral (flag wilson-gpu-provisioned). Money/destroy are double-gated by SEPARATE off-by-default switches: provisioning (up also needs --yes) and reaping (any strategy auto-down). fanout is a cost-tolerance decision aid for shardable jobs (on/off, OFF by default — never auto-executes). Inert unless runpodctl / vastai is on PATH. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_GPU=1.

plugins.name: wilson-decision-gate
plugins.description: Step-by-step decision gate — multi-decision work is one user-confirmation gate per decision, never batched: for each branch point present options + recommendation + rationale (3+ bullets), wait for the pick, then record a `### Decision N` block in design.md. Standalone port of wilson's principle-step-by-step-decision-gate (text-only, like wilson — a hook can't detect batching either). SessionStart **and PostCompact** inject the principle (PostCompact lands in the clean post-summary context — the rules survive compaction); UserPromptSubmit adds a short reminder ONLY on branch-point-looking prompts (not every prompt). /wilson-decision-gate maintains the design.md ledger; ships a 5-language canonical sample. An active native /goal flips the gate into autonomy mode — adopt the recommendation, log it, and continue instead of stopping for a pick (the /goal is standing authorization; PreToolUse safety guards still fire). Default ON; opt out: SIDECAR_NO_DECISION_GATE.

plugins.name: wilson-fire-gate
plugins.description: Instrument-first decision gate — measure over predict. A *fire* = running one real measurement (benchmark / profile / probe) instead of predicting its outcome. At a measure-vs-predict fork the agent should: (1) predict first with the most faithful model it has — prior measurements, roofline limits, documented walls; (2) fire only when that prediction is genuinely uncertain; (3) never re-fire a result a prior measurement already settled — resolve analytically ($0); (4) treat cost-no-object as 'do not block a needed fire on its cost', NOT as 'fire when the answer is already known'. Each fire-vs-resolve fork is gated (options + recommendation + 3+ rationale, wait for the pick) and recorded as a `### Fire-decision N` block in design.md. SessionStart + PostCompact inject the principle; UserPromptSubmit adds a short reminder only on measurement-looking prompts. An active native /goal flips the gate into autonomy mode — apply the four tenets, adopt the fire-vs-resolve call, log it, and continue instead of stopping (a /goal is standing authorization for the cost-bearing fires its closure needs). /wilson-fire-gate status|on|off|decide|log|path|sample. Companion to wilson-decision-gate (generic gate) — this one is measurement-specialized. Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_FIRE_GATE=1.

plugins.name: wilson-frontdoor
plugins.description: Inject the `frontdoor` operating principle (정공법 — take the orthodox path at a fork) as additionalContext. At a wall the agent should prefer the root-cause fix over a downstream workaround that papers over the symptom; when the root lives in an upstream / another SSOT repo it must NOT silently work around it here but escalate via `wilson-inbox` (a structured cross-repo handoff); a workaround is acceptable only as an explicitly recorded stopgap (decision-gate entry + TODO at the root), never the silent default. Pure text contributor — NO enforcement (a hook fires per tool/turn, not per *fork*, so it cannot detect the workaround-vs-root choice — same reason wilson-decision-gate is text-only); it keeps the principle in context so the model self-applies it. Two-tier cadence (the wilson-prefs / wilson-decision-gate pattern; a principle injected only at SessionStart fades structurally): FULL body on SessionStart + PostCompact, FULL refresh every Nth UserPromptSubmit turn (SIDECAR_FRONTDOOR_REFRESH, default 10), a compact one-line reminder on off-turns so it never decays to a bare label mid-session. Complements wilson-inbox (the escalation target) and wilson-hexa-first (hexa-path-specific; this one is language/project-general). Toggle: /sidecar off frontdoor. Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_FRONTDOOR=1 (or WILSON_NO_FRONTDOOR=1).

plugins.name: wilson-go
plugins.description: UserPromptSubmit hook — when the user types a bare AN10 fan-out keyword (`go` / `loop` / `병렬` / ...), inject an instruction telling Claude to dispatch multiple `Agent` tool calls in a SINGLE message, all with `run_in_background=true`, treating the prior user request as the fan-out task. Sidecar-minimal port of dancinlab/archive-nexus rule AN10 (rules/anima.json, added 2026-04-16) + tool/parallel_dispatch_guard.hexa — no agent-ledger, no DAG scheduler, no shadow comparator; just trigger detection and additionalContext injection. The model dispatches; the hook only nudges (same posture as wilson-frontdoor / wilson-decision-gate: a hook fires per turn, not per fork). Match is exact-after-strip+lower so `go to file X` does NOT trigger — only a bare keyword does. Trigger set: go · gogo · ㄱㄱ · 가자 · 가즈아 · keep · keep going · loop · 루프 · 병렬 · 동시 · 백그라운드. Default subagent_type mapping in the injected instruction: exploration→Explore, planning→Plan, general→general-purpose, claude-code/SDK questions→claude-code-guide. Anti-pattern called out: launch one Agent, wait for result, launch the next — sequential dispatch is exactly what AN10 prohibits. Toggle: /sidecar off go. Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_GO=1.

plugins.name: wilson-roi
plugins.description: UserPromptSubmit hook — when the user types a bare ROI keyword (`roi` / `ROI` / `투자대비` / `roi표`), inject a 5-column traffic-light table skeleton (등급 · id · 프로젝트 · 한 줄 · 효과) as additionalContext, telling Claude to fill it in this turn for the prior user context (option comparison / project ranking). Color enum: 🔴 Critical / 🟡 Important / 🟢 OK / ⚪ Backlog — sourced from archive-nexus tool/rules_loader.hexa::level_to_severity (cross-repo signal canon). Header label appears only on the first row of each color group; subsequent rows in the same group leave column-1 empty for visual grouping (the dashboard-tri pattern from archive-nexus docs/atlas_meta_dashboard.md, commit ab098d20 2026-04-22). Match is exact-after-strip+lower so `roi for X` does NOT trigger — only a bare keyword. Pairs with wilson-todo (same 5-col schema, backlog headers) and wilson-list (ordered next-up). Sidecar-minimal: no scoring engine, no persistence; the model fills the table. Toggle: /sidecar off roi. Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_ROI=1.

plugins.name: wilson-todo
plugins.description: UserPromptSubmit hook — when the user types a bare TODO keyword (`todo` / `TODO` / `할일` / `할 일`), inject a 5-column traffic-light table skeleton (우선 · id · 카테고리 · 설명 · 효과) as additionalContext, telling Claude to surface the pending work items from the prior user context. Color enum: 🔴 Critical / 🟡 Important / 🟢 OK / ⚪ Backlog (sidecar canon, archive-nexus tool/rules_loader.hexa::level_to_severity). Header label appears only on the first row of each color group; subsequent rows leave column-1 empty for visual grouping (the dashboard-tri pattern from archive-nexus docs/atlas_meta_dashboard.md, commit ab098d20 2026-04-22). Pairs with wilson-roi (same 5-col schema, option-comparison headers) and wilson-list (next-up ordered sequence). Match is exact-after-strip+lower so `todo for next sprint` does NOT trigger — only a bare keyword. Sidecar-minimal: no codebase scanner, no persistence; the model surfaces and fills the table from context. Toggle: /sidecar off todo. Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_TODO=1.

plugins.name: wilson-list
plugins.description: UserPromptSubmit hook — when the user types a bare LIST keyword (`list` / `리스트` / `다음` / `next` / `다음 할일`), inject an ordered-list skeleton (1./2./3.) as additionalContext, telling Claude to emit the next-up sequence of actions for the prior user context. Differs from wilson-todo (5-col color-tier table = backlog snapshot) and wilson-roi (5-col table = option comparison): wilson-list is a SIMPLE ORDERED SEQUENCE — what to do FIRST, what to do SECOND, what to do THIRD. No color column, no tier grouping; the order itself IS the priority signal. Default cap: 5-7 items. Match is exact-after-strip+lower so `list the files` does NOT trigger — only a bare keyword. Sidecar-minimal: no persistence; the model emits from current context. Toggle: /sidecar off list. Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_LIST=1.

plugins.name: wilson-tape-recorder
plugins.description: Record the Claude Code session as a `.tape` v1.2 execution trace (the dancinlab tape format — github.com/dancinlab/tape). One file per session at <DATA>/sessions/<id>.tape: SessionStart writes `@S start`, UserPromptSubmit writes `@U`, PreToolUse writes `@T`, PostToolUse writes `@R`, SessionEnd writes `@S end`. Honest subset of the 17-type alphabet — only what CC hooks actually deliver (no @A assistant text, no @K cost — those signals aren't exposed). Pairs with `wilson-guards/tape-append-only` (the recorder PRODUCES `.tape`, the guard PROTECTS it). /wilson-tape-recorder for status / ls / tail / cat / on / off. Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_TAPE_RECORDER=1.

plugins.name: wilson-goal
plugins.description: Session goal persistence + re-injection. Goal lives on disk at <DATA>/goal.json (outside the transcript that gets compacted), restored at every SessionStart **and PostCompact** (the clean post-summary moment) and re-asserted as a short reminder on each UserPromptSubmit. A project-local `GOAL.md` is used as the default when no user goal is set. /wilson-goal set|status|show|clear|path. HONEST GAP vs wilson's `loop` plugin: only the goal-persistence half is portable — the autonomous-continuation half (wilson's loop_tick + QUEUE auto-injection) cannot be done from CC hooks (no `continue without user input` mechanism). Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_GOAL=1.

plugins.name: wilson-resume
plugins.description: Persistence + re-arm companion for Claude Code's NATIVE /goal command (CC v2.1.139+). Native /goal sets a completion condition and keeps Claude working turn-to-turn until met, but the condition is session-scoped with NO disk persistence — a hard interruption (usage limit / rate-limit / crash / a closed terminal never cleanly --resume'd) loses it, and the next session has no idea a goal was ever active. wilson-resume is the small cog that closes that gap: on every Stop it scans the transcript for the most recent /goal command (a user message <command-name>/goal</command-name> + <command-args>CONDITION</command-args>) and persists the condition to a per-project file at <DATA>/<sha1(project)>.json; a bare /goal (empty args = the native clear) drops it. On SessionStart (source startup or clear — `resume` already carries the native goal over, `compact` is same-session) it injects a short `## Goal — interrupted` block naming the condition and the exact `/goal <condition>` line to re-arm it. Inert unless a /goal was actually used. Does not re-implement /goal — only persists what /goal cannot. /wilson-resume status|show|clear|path. Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_RESUME=1.

plugins.name: wilson-inbox
plugins.description: Cross-project handoff inbox — when a gap or request affects another SSOT repo, file it there as a structured inbox/<kind>/<slug>.md entry (kind: notes / patches / poc / rfc_drafts) instead of silently working around it downstream. /wilson-inbox:inbox scaffolds and manages entries: add / list / show / path / verify / apply / archive / rm / pr. The pr verb takes a scaffolded entry through the full upstream loop in one idempotent step: checkout (or create) `inbox/<kind>/<slug>` branch in the target repo, stage + commit the entry, push -u, then `gh pr create` with the file's first H1 as the PR title and the file body as the PR body. Target repo: --to <name> (~/core/<name>) or the nearest .git walking up from the cwd. Light-mode is folder + entries; heavy-mode adds inbox/PATCHES.yaml with a status lifecycle that apply/archive transition. A SessionStart hook surfaces the current repo's inbox entries so a handoff is never forgotten — inert unless the repo has an inbox/. Standalone port of wilson's inbox plugin. Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_INBOX=1.

plugins.name: wilson-lsp
plugins.description: Wires LSP servers for the dancinlab formats via .lsp.json: .hexa → `hexa lsp`; .tape / .n6 / .hxc / .kosmos → the canonical per-repo servers (tape-lsp / n6-lsp / hxc-lsp / kosmos-lsp, shipped in github.com/dancinlab/{tape,n6,hxc,kosmos}). Graceful: a server not on PATH just shows in /plugin Errors. Not a hook plugin — LSP lifecycle is CC-managed.

plugins.name: sidecar
plugins.description: Control plugin: /sidecar status|on|off <name> toggles the other sidecar plugins at runtime via a shared state file (~/.claude/sidecar/disabled.json) every plugin's hook checks. Persists across sessions; complements the native /plugin manager.

plugins.name: worktree-pr
plugins.description: Safe worktree -> PR -> merge -> cleanup workflow as the /worktree-pr:wt slash command. start makes an isolated worktree+branch off origin's default branch; ship pushes + opens a PR; finish merges the PR, removes the worktree, deletes the remote branch, refreshes the base. Never touches the main working tree or a concurrent session's branch.

plugins.name: wilson-sdlc
plugins.description: Spec → Plan → Build → Test&Recover → Review → Simplify → Ship — a 7-stage software-delivery skill pipeline. Skill *structure* is borrowed from addyosmani/agent-skills; the *content* is wilson's own dogfooded workflow, so each stage maps onto sidecar primitives the user already has: Spec→design.md+wilson-decision-gate, Plan→task tools+wilson-goal, Build→worktree-pr+wilson-checkpoint, Test&Recover→wilson-pool routing + systematic root-cause, Review→6-axis (5 generic + design.md decision-conformance), Simplify→sequences the built-in /simplify then re-checks decisions, Ship→the version→marketplace.json→GROWTH §10→commit checklist this very pack ships by. Stages that map to a wilson plugin degrade gracefully when that plugin is absent. Skills load on-demand; the plugin injects nothing on its own.

plugins.name: wilson-gap
plugins.description: /gap — multi-axis gap exploration. One slash command sweeps the current work through 40 breakthrough-strategy lenses curated into 8 families from the archived hive repo's state/*_audit catalogue (each lens = one probing one-liner; the catalogue is the single SSOT, inlined in the command body). Bare `/gap` runs mode C: inline-triage all 40 lenses against the current work, then dispatch a deep-dive subagent only for each family that surfaced a candidate gap — zero gaps spawns nothing (the fixpoint lens applied to /gap itself). `/gap full` runs mode A: fan-out one subagent per family with no triage, for an exhaustive audit. `/gap <text>` scopes the sweep. `/gap list` prints the 40-lens catalogue and exits. Surfaces and prioritises gaps only — never fixes; the user drives what gets fixed next. No hooks, no bin script — a pure model-facing command. Injects nothing until invoked.

plugins.name: wilson-schedule
plugins.description: A global daily-routine task list whose `run` action fans every pending task out to a background subagent in parallel. `/wilson-schedule:schedule add "<task>"` registers a free-text task; `list` shows the list; `run` — also triggered by typing the phrase `스케쥴 진행`, caught by a UserPromptSubmit hook — dispatches ALL pending tasks at once as background agents (one Agent call per task, run_in_background), per the immediate-full-fan-out model; `running`/`done` move a task's state, `done` capturing a one-line result; `rm`/`clear` prune. A CC hook/bin cannot call the Agent tool itself, so `run` only EMITS a dispatch directive — the bin owns state, the model owns the fan-out. State is a single global JSON file at the home root — ~/SCHEDULE.json (a day is a per-user unit, not per-repo). SessionStart surfaces a pending-count line. No remote cron — that is the built-in /schedule's job; this is a local immediate parallel executor. Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_SCHEDULE=1.

plugins.name: wilson-research
plugins.description: Research-fetch tools — pull the latest research material from arXiv and from YouTube. `/wilson-research:yt <url-or-id> [lang]` extracts a video's caption transcript (manual or auto-generated, any available language) by POSTing the InnerTube ANDROID player API and flattening the timedtext payload. `/wilson-research:arxiv <query|id> [--n N]` searches the official arXiv API or looks up a paper by id, returning title / authors / date / categories / pdf link / abstract. Pure Python stdlib — no binaries, no pip dependencies, no API key. Two model-invocable slash commands; no hooks.

plugins.name: wilson-chat-only
plugins.description: Deny the AskUserQuestion tool at the PreToolUse layer — the boxed terminal option-picker UI is forbidden, options must be presented inline as plain text and the agent waits for the user's plain-text reply (chat-only interaction mode). The deny reason is an AI-native single-line directive (CHAT_ONLY_MODE { rule, action, scope, optout }) so the model parses it and adapts the same turn, rather than retrying the tool. Solves the case where rigid options+rationale picker output reads as a wizard wall rather than a conversation. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_CHAT_ONLY=1 (or WILSON_NO_CHAT_ONLY=1).

plugins.name: wilson-hexa-first-warn
plugins.description: PreToolUse guard on Write when file_path ends in `.py` or `.sh` — acts on the hexa-first principle, asking whether a hexa-native equivalent (stdlib, atlas-bound theorem, or `hexa` CLI subcommand) covers the same job before a hand-rolled script lands. Two modes, selected by SIDECAR_HEXA_FIRST_WARN_MODE (WILSON_ accepted): `block` (DEFAULT) — the Write is DENIED at PreToolUse via permissionDecision, emitting an AI-native single-line directive (HEXA_FIRST_BLOCK { trigger, target, ext, action }); the model must pick a hexa-native path or downgrade. `warn` — a single-line advisory (HEXA_FIRST_WARN { ... }) is injected via additionalContext and the Write proceeds unchanged. Any value other than `warn` resolves to `block` — block is the floor. New-file moment only (`Write` matcher, NOT `Edit`) — editing an existing .py file does not nag every time. Whitelist is deliberately narrow (`.py`, `.sh`) — `.js`/`.ts`/`Makefile` etc. are out of scope to avoid noise. Soft enforcement complement to wilson-prefs's principle-text injection — text says "prefer hexa", this stops at the file-creation moment. Opt out entirely: SIDECAR_NO_HEXA_FIRST_WARN=1 (or WILSON_NO_HEXA_FIRST_WARN=1).

plugins.name: wilson-hexa-first
plugins.description: Inject the canonical `hexa-first` operating principle as additionalContext on SessionStart and PostCompact, so the agent reads it once at the top of context (and again right after a summary lands). Pure text contributor — NO enforcement (the soft enforcement complement is `wilson-hexa-first-warn`, which warns at .py/.sh write time). The principle text is the verbatim port of wilson `plugins/principle-hexa-first/main.hexa::principle_hexa_first_text` — prefer the hexa-native path over an external CLI / non-hexa dep, absorbed intrinsics over forking a shell, the absorbed stdlib / atlas over hand-rolling; when the constraint lives in hexa-lang itself fix it there PR-only via `hexa atlas register` → `hexa atlas pr` (direct fold-to-live forbidden). One block, two events (SessionStart + PostCompact). Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_HEXA_FIRST=1 (or WILSON_NO_HEXA_FIRST=1).

plugins.name: wilson-tree
plugins.description: Junk-drawer guard for AI agents in research repos. A project that fires N experiments tends to scatter them across a flat `state/<§N_…>/` tree plus an ever-growing inline progress log, so an agent that re-enters the repo has to re-grep the whole world to figure out where §107 lives or what its verdict was. wilson-tree teaches three habits at the hook layer. (P1 inject) SessionStart / PostCompact / UserPromptSubmit reads the project's `REGISTRY.md` (one line per §N: id · topic · status · path · verdict-slug) and the nearest `AGENTS.md` walk-up and injects a compact summary so the agent walks in with the map. (P2 register) PostToolUse(Write) on `state/**/result.json` auto-classifies the new §N by directory-name keyword substring against the configured topic list (ambiguous → UNCLASSIFIED), and appends a `REGISTRY.md` row. (P3 guard) PreToolUse(Write|Edit|MultiEdit) on `state/**` outside `<tree_root>/<topic>/<state_subdir>/` emits a one-line warning by default; `SIDECAR_TREE_GUARD_STRICT=1` upgrades it to a deny. Inert until the repo has either a `REGISTRY.md` or a `.wilson-tree.json` — a fresh repo sees zero hook side effects. Project-local config at `<repo-root>/.wilson-tree.json` (tree_root / state_subdir / topics / strict / inject_short_max). Slash: /wilson-tree:tree status|init|audit|topics|register|path|off. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_TREE=1.
